title: $occcrc
html:
$occcrc

$occcrc

Set or return the CRC checksum of an occurrence.

$occcrc ( Entity )

$occcrc ( Entity ) = CheckSum

Parameters

Table 1. Parameters
Parameter Data Type Description

Entity

String Entity name

CheckSum

String Check sum can be set to 00000000 to disable the CRC check.

Return Values

  • Eight-character hexadecimal string that represents the current values of an occurrence's fields. It is the CRC checksum as calculated by the database driver for database occurrences.
  • An empty string for non-database occurrences.

If $occcrc is not equal to an eight-character hexadecimal string, $procerror is set to the error constant <UPROCERR_RANGE>.

Use

Allowed in all component types.

Description

$occcrc returns an eight-character hexadecimal string that represents the current values of an occurrence's fields. If the occurrence's field values change, the CRC checksum calculation yields a different result.

The value can be set by $occcrc, and by ProcScript statements that load data from a disconnected record set: xmload, webload, and structToComponent. These commands set the CRC to match the value in the disconnected record set. Otherwise, $occcrc is set automatically when loading data from a database.

Note: The CRC is not set if the entity's Locking property is set to No Updates.

Disconnected Record Sets

CRC checksum values are required for disconnected record sets, and are produced automatically by Uniface when loading data from a database, or when creating or loading data from XML, JSON, or Structs. They are used by reconnect process initiated by reconnect or retrieve/reconnect to determine if an occurrence can be updated by a disconnected occurrence. The reconnect process does not update an occurrence unless the CRC value in the data stream matches the value of $occcrc for the occurrence in the component.

Disabling CRC Checks During retrieve/reconnect

If you set the value of $occcrc for an occurrence to "00000000" then retrieve/reconnect does not carry out a CRC check before merging data from the XML stream into the occurrence.

Related Links
  • Disconnected Record Sets

    Disconnected record sets provide a way for components to maintain and share data and its state (such as whether the data has been modified) even when a connection to a database is not available. They are typically used for communication between web components (server pages and web services) and are usually implemented as XML, JSON, or Uniface Structs.

  • ProcScript for Disconnected Record Sets

    Uniface supports XML, JSON, and Uniface Structs for disconnected record sets and provides specialized ProcScript instructions for saving, loading, reconnecting and querying data from disconnected record sets.

  • Occurrence Metadata for Reconnect

    To manage the exchange of data between components and a database, metadata is maintained about occurrences, such as whether the occurrence is new or already stored in a database. This occurs transparently, so that a programmer generally does not have to handle data state.

  • Reconnect Process

    The reconnect process merges data in disconnected occurrences (in XML, JSON, or Structs) with data in the component, and resolves the state information that is stored in the modification status attributes. It removes duplicates of occurrences, removes occurrences marked for deletion from the component, and sets the appropriate modification flags.
